text.title: Asset Harvest Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that prevent the harvest from rotting into broken-asset clutter or worse — accidental client-IP exfiltration.

rule.statement: The harvest pass MUST be done WITHIN 14 DAYS of the sunset decision. Context decays fast: by day 30 the indie does not remember which modules worked, which were brittle, which scripts mattered. The 14-day window is hard.
rule.rationale: Indies who delay harvest beyond 30 days harvest at <30% the asset rate of those who do it within 14 days. The asset isn't the code — it's the indie's institutional knowledge about which code was load-bearing.

rule.statement: Every harvested file MUST carry an SPDX license header. The starter kit declares its overall license; assets that disagree (e.g. AGPL dependency, GPL fragment) MUST be split into a separately-licensed folder OR NOT harvested. License-by-default-is-fine is REJECTED.

rationale.source: SPDX usage in production; license-audit incidents

rule.rationale: A starter kit with mixed-license code is a litigation surface. When the next bet uses an unlicensed asset and ships to a customer, the indie has no defence. Per-file license headers are the cheapest insurance.

rule.statement: NO code, copy, schema, or asset written under client engagement OR with contractor obligations is permitted in the starter kit. Even code the indie originally wrote that was paid-for is OUT. Harvest from indie-owned products only.
rule.rationale: Work-for-hire transfers IP. Putting client work into a personal starter kit is a contractual breach that surfaces when the indie wins a contract from a competing client. Hard line, no exceptions.

rule.statement: Harvest in this order: (1) schema (DB / API contract / data shapes), (2) deploy + infra, (3) copy + illustrations, (4) UI components, (5) lib / utility modules, (6) feature code. Schema-first because it's the slowest to rebuild and the most reusable; feature-specific code last because it's least likely to generalise.
rule.rationale: Indies report schema + deploy as the highest-leverage harvest classes: a billing schema reused across 3 products saves 2-3 weeks per bet. Feature code is bet-specific; harvest only when it's clearly reusable.

rule.statement: Every extraction MUST be logged in `harvest-log.md` with: source product, source file, destination, generalisation level (verbatim / parameterised / heavily-rewritten), and a one-line "lesson" from the original use. Empty / undocumented assets in the starter kit are REJECTED.
rule.rationale: Assets without provenance / lesson get reused blindly; the next bet hits the same bug. Logged lessons make the starter kit smarter over time — each harvest accumulates wisdom, not just code.

section.title: The 7-section checklist (summary)

list.item: S1 schema: DB migrations, API contracts, JSON schemas.
S2 deploy: deploy scripts, CI configs, dockerfiles, env-var templates.
S3 infra: Terraform modules, k8s manifests, secret-management patterns.
S4 copy: landing-page hero variants, FAQ blocks, onboarding emails, support replies.
S5 UI: reusable components (auth forms, billing pages, dashboards, settings panels).
S6 lib: utility modules (auth helpers, billing webhook handlers, queue wrappers).
S7 illustrations: SVG / Lottie / generated images you own.

section.reference: Folder conventions and intended contents per section.

example.title: Harvest log entry with lesson
example.code:
## 2026-04-12 — Sunset: shipsuit.io
### S6 lib — billing-webhook-handler - Source: shipsuit/src/server/webhooks/stripe.ts - Destination: starter-kit/lib/stripe-webhooks/ - Generalisation: parameterised (extracted price IDs and product slugs to env vars) - Lesson: ALWAYS verify webhook signature in middleware, not in route handler. The bet shipped to prod without verification for 2 days; one fraudulent webhook later, we learned. Verifier middleware is now in starter kit.
example.note: r5 satisfied. Provenance + generalisation level + lesson. Next reader of starter-kit/lib/stripe-webhooks/ learns the lesson without re-incurring the cost.

example.title: Client-code accidentally harvested
example.code: ## 2026-04-12 — Sunset: bookings-app ### S5 UI — booking-calendar-widget - Source: client-contract-acme/src/widgets/Calendar.tsx - Destination: starter-kit/ui/calendar/ - Lesson: works great.
example.note: r3 violation. Source path reveals the asset was written under a client contract. Hard stop: remove from starter kit, scrub git history of the harvest commit.
